source.definition: Used to indicate that something is true or will happen regardless of other circumstances or objections.

explanation.sourceText: The English word 'anyway' is formed from 'any' and 'way', meaning 'in any manner' or 'regardless'.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „anyway” este format din 'any' și 'way', însemnând 'în orice mod' sau 'indiferent'.

target.definition: Drückt aus, dass etwas auf jeden Fall oder jedenfalls so ist, unabhängig von anderen Umständen.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word 'oricum' is composed of 'ori' (or) and 'cum' (how), meaning 'anyhow' or 'in any case'.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „oricum” este compus din 'ori' (sau) și 'cum' (cum), însemnând 'oricum', 'în orice caz'.

examples.sourceText: I will come anyway to the party.
Anyway, that is a good idea.
It will rain, but I will go for a walk anyway.
examples.targetText: Va ploua, dar oricum merg la plimbare.

learningTips.text: Remember 'oricum' by thinking of 'ori' as 'or' and 'cum' as 'how' — so it means 'anyhow' or 'anyway'.
The English word 'anyway' sounds like 'oricum' in Romanian, both meaning 'regardless' or 'in any case'.
Imagine saying 'I will come oricum' — no matter what, I will come, just like 'anyway' in English.
Link 'anyway' with 'oricum' because both express that something is true or happens regardless of other factors.
